title: 5 Viewing and Tracing Incoming Clearing Items
body_text:
5	Viewing and Tracing Incoming Clearing Items
5.1	Overview of Viewing and Tracing Incoming Clearing Items
Credit unions can search for, view, and print Incoming Clearing Items via ORS. If possible, use ORS to resolve inquiries and print copies because it is faster and more cost-efficient than sending a trace request to Central 1.
5.2	Viewing and Printing Incoming Clearing Items via ORS
Central 1 captures images of Qualified Incoming Clearing Items and makes them available to credit unions via ORS. The clearing reports (CLI, CLO, and CLUS) provide an account number, trace number, and amount for each item. This information can be used to view specific items on ORS.
Since physical cheques arenГўВҖВҷt provided to the credit unions, use the images in ORS to:
Validate cheques drawn on the credit union and on its membersГўВҖВҷ accounts
Note:	Credit unions that have a large volume of items may establish a policy to verify items only over a certain dollar value.
Make return decisions
Return cheques
Reinstate cheques (i.e., update incorrect cheque data such as the Transit Number, account number, or cheque number. The correct information would then be included in the Image File for member statements).
Research inquiries (i.e., use ORS to resolve trace inquiries where an adjustment isnГўВҖВҷt required. Although most inquiries can be resolved via ORS, submit a trace request to Central 1 if required).
ORS retains a history of cheque images for seven years. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures on using ORS.
5.3	Tracing Incoming Clearing Items
If an inquiry on a Canadian or US dollar Incoming Clearing Item cannot be resolved via ORS or if a financial adjustment is required, complete a trace request on ServiceNow.
Note:	If possible, use ORS to resolve inquiries and print copies because it is faster and more cost-efficient than sending a trace request to Central 1.
To complete a manual trace request, proceed as follows:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	YouГўВҖВҷll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itГўВҖВҷs your first login to the Client Centre, youГўВҖВҷve deleted your cookies, or youГўВҖВҷre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
ServiceNow.
See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youГўВҖВҷve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under ГўВҖВңMy FavouritesГўВҖВқ.
Figure 3: Accessing ServiceNow
Select
Service Catalogue
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Select Service Catalogue
Select
Paper Payments
and then select
Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Select Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request
Complete the Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request form as follows. See Figure 6.
Note: 	Mandatory fields have an asterisk (*).
ГўВ‘
Enter the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs transit number. If applicable, enter the transit number that will be debited for the trace request in the Transit Number to Charge fields (e.g., head office).
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Use the drop-down menu to select the type of trace.
ГўВ‘Вў
Select the currency of the item.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Enter the amount of the item.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Provide a detailed description.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Attach any supporting information, if applicable.
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Select
Submit
. The request is sent to Central 1 and a ServiceNow ticket number is created.
Figure 6: The Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request Form
Proceed as follows if ServiceNow isnГўВҖВҷt available:
Complete the Trace Request ГўВҖВ“ Clearing and Remittance Items, Form 1550. See Figure 7. Completion of this form is self-explanatory.
Note:	DonГўВҖВҷt use this form to trace electronic transactions such as AFT, electronic bill payments, funds transfers, etc.
Figure 7: Trace Request ГўВҖВ“ Clearing and Remittance Items, Form 1550
Obtain copies of the support documents as described in the Type of Trace section of the form (e.g., a copy of the CLI, CLO, or CLUS report).
Fax the form and the support documents as described on the form.
Once Central 1 completes the trace request, a response will be sent to the credit union. If applicable, a service charge will be debited to the credit unionГўВҖВҷs account.
Related Information
Trace Request ГўВҖВ“ Clearing and Remittance Items,
Form 1550
END
